title: Claude just dropped UltraCode... its Insane
summary: Claude released UltraCode, a feature that enables up to 10 parallel agents managed by code instead of a master LLM. The tutorial demonstrates practical patterns like judge panels, adversarial verification, deterministic fan out, and dynamic model selection via OpenRouter for agentic systems.
writeup: The video covers Claude's UltraCode, a feature that runs up to 10 parallel agents coordinated by code rather than a master LLM. The tutorial walks through practical patterns for agentic systems: judge panels, adversarial verification, deterministic fan out, and dynamic model selection through OpenRouter. The shift from an orchestrating LLM to code-managed coordination matters for building multi-agent workflows with predictable control. Key takeaways include how to structure parallel agents and route across different models at runtime.
